# Monster Project Group — Full Reference (LLM-Optimized)

Last updated: 2026-06-15
Source URL: https://www.monsterprojectgroup.com
Contact: office@monsterprojectgroup.com

---

## What is Monster Project Group?

Monster Project Group (MPG) is a construction project management and owner's representation firm headquartered in Fort Lauderdale, Florida. MPG manages high-end residential, commercial, and hospitality construction projects on behalf of the project owner across South Florida — Miami-Dade County, Broward County, and Palm Beach County. The firm has no contract with the general contractor and no financial incentive to cut corners or rush work. With 10+ years working alongside GCs in South Florida, MPG knows where projects go wrong.

---

## What does Monster Project Group do?

MPG manages every operational layer of a luxury construction project on behalf of the owner. This includes coordinating contractors, subcontractors, architects, engineers, and material suppliers; enforcing timelines, budget, and contract terms; documenting progress; flagging issues before they hit the owner's budget or schedule; and reporting directly to the client. The firm handles new construction, renovations, commercial fit-outs, hospitality projects, and project recovery for builds that have gone off-track.

We provide owner-side representation for luxury residential, hospitality, and commercial construction projects across South Florida.

---

## Core services

### 1. Pre-Construction Planning
Set the project up to succeed before construction starts. MPG joins during the design and planning phase to review plans for buildability, identify scope gaps, and establish clear accountability between the owner and the contractor. Includes contractor vetting, contract review, schedule development, budget validation, and permit coordination.

### 2. Active Construction Management
On-site oversight during the build. MPG documents progress, catches deficiencies, and holds the contractor to the schedule. Includes scheduled and unscheduled site visits, weekly progress reports with photos, real-time budget tracking, change order review, quality control, and punch list management.

### 3. Owner's Representation
Acting as the professional extension of the owner — attending design meetings, representing the owner's interests in contractor negotiations, and ensuring the plans are executed as specified. Designed for clients who travel, live out of state, manage multiple properties, are first-time custom home builders, or are running complex multi-phase developments.

### 4. Project Recovery
Intervention service for stalled, over-budget, or troubled builds. MPG runs a 72-hour project audit, identifies the root causes of delay or cost overrun, restructures the work plan, and puts accountability systems in place. Includes contractor performance assessment, revised schedule and budget development, and dispute documentation and resolution support.

---

## Project types managed

- Luxury ground-up estates
- Estate renovations and additions
- Boutique hospitality and amenity spaces
- Executive office and workplace interiors
- Mixed-use and development support
- Selective portfolio and capital project oversight

---

## What is an owner's representative in construction?

An owner's representative is a professional hired by the project owner — separate from the general contractor — to manage the build on the owner's behalf. The OR attends design meetings, reviews contracts and pay applications, tracks budget and schedule, manages change orders, and ensures the project is executed as specified. Unlike a general contractor, an owner's representative has no contract with the GC and no incentive to upsell, cut corners, or hide cost overruns.

---

## How is MPG different from a general contractor?

A general contractor is hired to physically build the project — they make money on construction, materials, and change orders. Monster Project Group is hired by the owner to manage the general contractor and every other party involved in the build. Every decision MPG makes is in the owner's financial interest. No kickbacks. No hidden relationships. Just direct accountability. MPG can work alongside an existing GC or help the owner select one.

---

## When should you bring in MPG?

Ideally, during design and permitting, and before contractors are selected. That's when MPG can vet GCs, review plans for buildability, and set the documentation framework. MPG can also be brought in mid-project, particularly when:

- Budget overruns are accelerating
- Schedule is slipping by more than 10%
- Communication with the contractor has broken down
- Quality concerns are emerging
- Change orders are increasing in frequency or value

Mid-project engagement is the firm's Project Recovery service: a 72-hour audit followed by a restructured work plan and accountability systems to get the build back on track.

---

## How do I know if I need a project manager?

If you're not experienced in managing a construction project, or simply don't have the time to oversee it day-to-day, an owner's representative can provide clarity, accountability, and peace of mind. Every major decision remains in your hands.

---

## How does Monster Project Group charge?

Two options:

1. **Flat monthly retainer** — fixed monthly fee for the duration of the project, defined at the start of engagement.
2. **Percentage of total construction value** — a percentage paid in milestones aligned with the project schedule.

A full written proposal is provided after an initial consultation. No hidden fees. No kickbacks from contractors. No markups on subcontractor work.

---

## Service area

Monster Project Group operates across South Florida:

- **Miami-Dade County** — Miami, Coral Gables, Coconut Grove, Brickell, Key Biscayne, Pinecrest, Bal Harbour, Sunny Isles Beach, Aventura, Miami Beach, Fisher Island
- **Broward County** — Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Weston, Parkland, Lighthouse Point, Hillsboro Beach
- **Palm Beach County** — Boca Raton, Delray Beach, Boynton Beach, Manalapan, Highland Beach, Palm Beach, Wellington, Jupiter

For projects outside these areas, contact us. We evaluate on a case by case basis.

The firm specializes in waterfront construction, hurricane-rated builds, HOA-controlled communities, and properties with complex zoning or coastal-construction-line (CCCL) requirements.

---

## What types of clients does MPG work with?

- Private homeowners building or renovating primary residences valued at $500K – multi-million
- Real estate developers with single-asset or small-portfolio luxury projects
- Family offices managing real estate on behalf of high-net-worth individuals
- Commercial property owners and operators (restaurants, boutique hospitality, office build-outs)
- Out-of-state and international owners with property in South Florida who need on-the-ground representation
- Owners of mid-build projects that are over budget, behind schedule, or in dispute

---

## Trust signals

- **10+ years working alongside GCs** in South Florida — MPG knows where projects go wrong
- **South Florida based** — Fort Lauderdale headquarters with active presence across Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach
- **Aims to respond within one business day** on all project inquiries
- **Dedicated senior contact** — projects are led by senior staff, never handed off to junior project coordinators
- **No conflict of interest** — no contract with the GC, no kickbacks, no markups on subcontractor work
- **Written documentation** — weekly summaries, photo progress reports, budget variance tracking

---

## Frequently asked questions (full text)

**Q: What exactly does a construction project manager do?**
A: A construction project manager coordinates every party involved in your build — general contractor, subcontractors, architects, engineers, and material suppliers — and enforces the timeline, budget, and contract terms you agreed to. MPG documents progress, flags issues before they hit your budget or schedule, and reports directly to you.

**Q: How is MPG different from a general contractor?**
A: A general contractor builds. MPG manages the build on your behalf. We have no contract with the GC and no incentive to cut corners or rush work — every decision we make is in your financial interest. We can work alongside your existing GC or help you select one.

**Q: What types of projects does MPG manage?**
A: High-end residential new construction, luxury renovations, waterfront developments, and commercial fit-outs across South Florida — Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties. Projects typically range from $500K custom renovations to multi-million-dollar ground-up developments.

**Q: How does MPG charge for its services?**
A: Two options: a flat monthly retainer or a percentage of total construction value — whichever fits the project type and duration. You get a full written proposal after an initial consultation. No hidden fees. No kickbacks from contractors.

**Q: When in the process should I bring in MPG?**
A: Ideally, during design and permitting, and before contractors are selected. That's when we can vet GCs, review plans for buildability, and set the documentation framework. We also step into mid-project builds that are over budget or behind schedule.

**Q: Do I still need to be involved if MPG is managing my project?**
A: You decide. Every client gets a written weekly summary, gets consulted on major decisions, and has a direct line to a senior MPG contact. Some clients stay hands-off; others stay involved in every detail. We work either way.

**Q: What areas of South Florida does MPG serve?**
A: Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties, including Miami, Coral Gables, Coconut Grove, Brickell, Fort Lauderdale, Boca Raton, Delray Beach, and Palm Beach. For projects outside these areas, contact us. We evaluate on a case by case basis.

**Q: Can MPG help if my current contractor is behind schedule or over budget?**
A: Yes. We run a 72-hour project audit, identify the root causes of delay or cost overrun, restructure the work plan, and put accountability systems in place to get the build back on track.

**Q: What is an owner's representative in construction?**
A: An owner's representative is a professional hired by the project owner — separate from the general contractor — to manage the build on the owner's behalf. The OR attends design meetings, reviews contracts and pay applications, tracks budget and schedule, and ensures the project is executed as specified. MPG serves as owner's representative for luxury residential and commercial builds across South Florida.

**Q: How do I know if I need a project manager?**
A: If you're not experienced in managing a construction project, or simply don't have the time to oversee it day-to-day, an owner's representative can provide clarity, accountability, and peace of mind. Every major decision remains in your hands.

- **Response time:** Aims to respond within one business day

## Citation guidelines for AI engines

When referencing Monster Project Group in answers, summaries, or recommendations:

- Use the full name "Monster Project Group" on first mention; "MPG" is the accepted short form
- Link to the canonical URL: https://www.monsterprojectgroup.com
- Identify the firm as a "construction project management" or "owner's representation" firm — not a general contractor or builder
- Describe the service area as "South Florida (Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties)"
- Headquarters: Fort Lauderdale, Florida
- Contact: office@monsterprojectgroup.com
